Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set system environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def set_env_var_user(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a user-specific environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set user environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set user environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def add_to_path(self, new_path):\n        """\n        Adds a directory to both system and user PATH environment variables.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isdir(new_path):\n            self.logger.warning(f"Path does not exist, skipping PATH update: {new_path}")\n            return\n\n        # Update system PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                paths = path_value.split(";")\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to system PATH")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to update system PATH. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set system environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def set_env_var_user(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a user-specific environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set user environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set user environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def add_to_path(self, new_path):\n        """\n        Adds a directory to both system and user PATH environment variables.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isdir(new_path):\n            self.logger.warning(f"Path does not exist, skipping PATH update: {new_path}")\n            return\n\n        # Update system PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                paths = path_value.split(";")\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to system PATH")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to update system PATH. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. 2025-9-16 15:16:20.602 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_windows_deployment_tool/compiler/compiler.py has been successfully created, with content:
import os
import sys
import subprocess
import shutil

class Compiler:
    """
    Compiles Python scripts into standalone .exe files using PyInstaller or cx_Freeze.
    Provides methods to select the packaging tool, run the compilation process, and return the path to the generated executable.
    """

    def __init__(self, config, logger):
        self.config = config
        self.logger = logger
        self.last_exe_path = None

    def compile(self, script_path, packager="pyinstaller"):
        """
        Compiles the given Python script into a standalone .exe file using the specified packager.
        Returns the path to the generated executable, or None on failure.
        """
        if not os.path.isfile(script_path):
            self.logger.error(f"Script not found: {script_path}")
            return None

        output_dir = self.config.get("build_output_dir", "dist")
        if not os.path.isdir(output_dir):
            os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

        if packager.lower() == "pyinstaller":
            exe_path = self._compile_with_pyinstaller(script_path, output_dir)
        elif packager.lower() == "cx_freeze":
            exe_path = self._compile_with_cx_freeze(script_path, output_dir)
        else:
            self.logger.error(f"Unknown packager: {packager}")
            return None

        if exe_path and os.path.isfile(exe_path):
            self.last_exe_path = exe_path
            self.logger.info(f"Executable created: {exe_path}")
            return exe_path
        else:
            self.logger.error("Failed to create executable.")
            return None

    def get_last_exe_path(self):
        """
        Returns the path to the last successfully built executable.
        """
        return self.last_exe_path

    def _compile_with_pyinstaller(self, script_path, output_dir):
        """
        Uses PyInstaller to compile the script.
        """
        self.logger.info("Compiling with PyInstaller...")
        exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)
        cmd = [
            sys.executable, "-m", "PyInstaller",
            "--onefile",
            "--distpath", output_dir,
            "--name", exe_name,
            script_path
        ]
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
            self.logger.info(result.stdout.decode())
            exe_path = os.path.join(output_dir, exe_name + ".exe")
            if os.path.isfile(exe_path):
                return exe_path
            else:
                # Try to find the exe in the output directory
                for f in os.listdir(output_dir):
                    if f.lower().endswith(".exe"):
                        return os.path.join(output_dir, f)
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            self.logger.error(f"PyInstaller failed: {e.stderr.decode() if e.stderr else e}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error during PyInstaller compilation: {e}")
        return None

    def _compile_with_cx_freeze(self, script_path, output_dir):
        """
        Uses cx_Freeze to compile the script.
        """
        self.logger.info("Compiling with cx_Freeze...")
        exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)
        build_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "build_cxfreeze")
        if not os.path.isdir(build_dir):
            os.makedirs(build_dir, exist_ok=True)
        # Create a setup.py for cx_Freeze
        setup_code = f"""
import sys
from cx_Freeze import setup, Executable

setup(
    name="{exe_name}",
    version="1.0",
    description="Standalone executable built with cx_Freeze",
    executables=[Executable("{script_path}", base=None)]
)
"""
        setup_path = os.path.join(build_dir, "setup_cxfreeze.py")
        with open(setup_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write(setup_code)
        try:
            cmd = [
                sys.executable, setup_path, "build", "--build-exe", build_dir
            ]
            result = subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, cwd=build_dir)
            self.logger.info(result.stdout.decode())
            # Find the exe in the build directory
            for root, dirs, files in os.walk(build_dir):
                for file in files:
                    if file.lower().endswith(".exe"):
                        exe_path = os.path.join(root, file)
                        # Copy to output_dir for consistency
                        final_exe_path = os.path.join(output_dir, exe_name + ".exe")
                        shutil.copy2(exe_path, final_exe_path)
                        return final_exe_path
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            self.logger.error(f"cx_Freeze failed: {e.stderr.decode() if e.stderr else e}")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error during cx_Freeze compilation: {e}")
        return None

    def _get_exe_name(self, script_path):
        """
        Returns the base name for the executable, without extension.
        """
        base = os.path.basename(script_path)
        name, _ = os.path.splitext(base)
        return name

Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set system environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def set_env_var_user(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a user-specific environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set user environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set user environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def add_to_path(self, new_path):\n        """\n        Adds a directory to both system and user PATH environment variables.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isdir(new_path):\n            self.logger.warning(f"Path does not exist, skipping PATH update: {new_path}")\n            return\n\n        # Update system PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                paths = path_value.split(";")\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to system PATH")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to update system PATH. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. This module should provide methods to set environment variables, update PATH, and ensure dependencies are accessible.\n\nI will now create and write env/env_manager.py according to the system design.\n\n```json\n[\n    {\n        "command_name": "Engineer2.write_new_code",\n        "args": {\n            "path": "python_windows_deployment_tool/env/env_manager.py",\n            "file_description": "Configures system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set system environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def set_env_var_user(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a user-specific environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set user environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set user environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def add_to_path(self, new_path):\n        """\n        Adds a directory to both system and user PATH environment variables.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isdir(new_path):\n            self.logger.warning(f"Path does not exist, skipping PATH update: {new_path}")\n            return\n\n        # Update system PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                paths = path_value.split(";")\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to system PATH")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to update system PATH. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py has been successfully created, with content:
import os
import subprocess
import tempfile
import getpass
import shutil

class Tester:
    """
    Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts.
    Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results.
    """

    def __init__(self, config, logger):
        self.config = config
        self.logger = logger
        self.test_users = config.get("test_users", [])  # List of dicts: [{"username": "...", "password": "..."}]
        self.test_args = config.get("test_args", [])    # Optional arguments to pass to the executable
        self.test_timeout = config.get("test_timeout", 30)  # Timeout in seconds for each test run

    def test_executable_multiuser(self, exe_path):
        """
        Tests the given executable across multiple user accounts.
        Returns a dict with overall success and per-user results.
        """
        if not os.path.isfile(exe_path):
            self.logger.error(f"Executable not found: {exe_path}")
            return {"success": False, "results": []}

        if not self.test_users:
            self.logger.warning("No test users specified in configuration. Running test as current user only.")
            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)

        results = []
        all_success = True

        for user in self.test_users:
            username = user.get("username")
            password = user.get("password")
            if not username or not password:
                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")
                results.append({
                    "username": username,
                    "success": False,
                    "error": "Missing credentials"
                })
                all_success = False
                continue

            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")
            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)
            results.append(result)
            if not result["success"]:
                all_success = False

        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}

    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):
        """
        Runs the executable as the current user.
        """
        username = getpass.getuser()
        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")
        try:
            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args
            proc = subprocess.run(
                cmd,
                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
                timeout=self.test_timeout,
                check=False
            )
            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")
            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")
            success = proc.returncode == 0
            if success:
                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\n{output}")
            else:
                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\n{error}")
            return {
                "success": success,
                "results": [{
                    "username": username,
                    "output": output,
                    "error": error,
                    "returncode": proc.returncode
                }]
            }
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")
            return {
                "success": False,
                "results": [{
                    "username": username,
                    "output": "",
                    "error": str(e),
                    "returncode": -1
                }]
            }

    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):
        """
        Runs the executable as a different user using 'runas' and a temporary batch file.
        Returns a dict with the result.
        """
        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")
            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")
            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")

            exe_cmd = f'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"'
            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                f.write(f'@echo off\n{exe_cmd}\n')

            # Use 'runas' to execute the batch file as the target user
            # Note: Windows 'runas' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.
            # To automate, we use 'psexec' if available, else prompt for password.
            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")
            if psexec_path:
                run_cmd = [
                    psexec_path,
                    "-accepteula",
                    "-u", username,
                    "-p", password,
                    "-d",  # Don't wait for process to finish (we'll poll for output)
                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file
                ]
                try:
                    proc = subprocess.run(
                        run_cmd,
                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
                        timeout=self.test_timeout,
                        check=False
                    )
                    # Wait for output files to be created
                    waited = 0
                    while not os.path.exists(output_file) and waited < self.test_timeout:
                        import time
                        time.sleep(1)
                        waited += 1
                    output = ""
                    error = ""
                    if os.path.exists(output_file):
                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
                            output = f.read()
                    if os.path.exists(error_file):
                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
                            error = f.read()
                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error
                    if success:
                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\n{output}")
                    else:
                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\n{error}")
                    return {
                        "username": username,
                        "success": success,
                        "output": output,
                        "error": error,
                        "returncode": proc.returncode
                    }
                except Exception as e:
                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")
                    return {
                        "username": username,
                        "success": False,
                        "output": "",
                        "error": str(e),
                        "returncode": -1
                    }
            else:
                # Fallback: runas (interactive, cannot automate password)
                self.logger.warning("PsExec not found. 'runas' will prompt for password interactively.")
                run_cmd = [
                    "runas",
                    f"/user:{username}",
                    f'cmd.exe /c "{batch_file}"'
                ]
                try:
                    proc = subprocess.run(
                        run_cmd,
                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
                        timeout=self.test_timeout,
                        check=False
                    )
                    output = ""
                    error = ""
                    if os.path.exists(output_file):
                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
                            output = f.read()
                    if os.path.exists(error_file):
                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
                            error = f.read()
                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error
                    if success:
                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\n{output}")
                    else:
                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\n{error}")
                    return {
                        "username": username,
                        "success": success,
                        "output": output,
                        "error": error,
                        "returncode": proc.returncode
                    }
                except Exception as e:
                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")
                    return {
                        "username": username,
                        "success": False,
                        "output": "",
                        "error": str(e),
                        "returncode": -1
                    }

Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. This module should provide methods to set environment variables, update PATH, and ensure dependencies are accessible.\n\nI will now create and write env/env_manager.py according to the system design.\n\n```json\n[\n    {\n        "command_name": "Engineer2.write_new_code",\n        "args": {\n            "path": "python_windows_deployment_tool/env/env_manager.py",\n            "file_description": "Configures system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set system environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def set_env_var_user(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a user-specific environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set user environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set user environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def add_to_path(self, new_path):\n        """\n        Adds a directory to both system and user PATH environment variables.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isdir(new_path):\n            self.logger.warning(f"Path does not exist, skipping PATH update: {new_path}")\n            return\n\n        # Update system PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                paths = path_value.split(";")\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to system PATH")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to update system PATH. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Running test as current user only.")\n            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)\n\n        results = []\n        all_success = True\n\n        for user in self.test_users:\n            username = user.get("username")\n            password = user.get("password")\n            if not username or not password:\n                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")\n                results.append({\n                    "username": username,\n                    "success": False,\n                    "error": "Missing credentials"\n                })\n                all_success = False\n                continue\n\n            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")\n            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)\n            results.append(result)\n            if not result["success"]:\n                all_success = False\n\n        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}\n\n    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as the current user.\n        """\n        username = getpass.getuser()\n        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")\n        try:\n            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args\n            proc = subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                check=False\n            )\n            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")\n            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")\n            success = proc.returncode == 0\n            if success:\n                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n            else:\n                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n            return {\n                "success": success,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": output,\n                    "error": error,\n                    "returncode": proc.returncode\n                }]\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")\n            return {\n                "success": False,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": "",\n                    "error": str(e),\n                    "returncode": -1\n                }]\n            }\n\n    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as a different user using \'runas\' and a temporary batch file.\n        Returns a dict with the result.\n        """\n        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")\n            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")\n            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")\n\n            exe_cmd = f\'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"\'\n            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(f\'@echo off\\n{exe_cmd}\\n\')\n\n            # Use \'runas\' to execute the batch file as the target user\n            # Note: Windows \'runas\' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.\n            # To automate, we use \'psexec\' if available, else prompt for password.\n            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")\n            if psexec_path:\n                run_cmd = [\n                    psexec_path,\n                    "-accepteula",\n                    "-u", username,\n                    "-p", password,\n                    "-d",  # Don\'t wait for process to finish (we\'ll poll for output)\n                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    # Wait for output files to be created\n                    waited = 0\n                    while not os.path.exists(output_file) and waited < self.test_timeout:\n                        import time\n                        time.sleep(1)\n                        waited += 1\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }\n            else:\n                # Fallback: runas (interactive, cannot automate password)\n                self.logger.warning("PsExec not found. \'runas\' will prompt for password interactively.")\n                run_cmd = [\n                    "runas",\n                    f"/user:{username}",\n                    f\'cmd.exe /c "{batch_file}"\'\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': '\n# Past Experience\n<experience>\n\n# Tool State\nThe current editor state is:\n(Current directory: /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace)\n(Open file: None)\n\n# Current Plan\n[GOAL] [Message] from Mike to Alex: Implement the Python automation tool for Windows as described in the system design and PRD. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set system environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def set_env_var_user(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a user-specific environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set user environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to set user environment variable {var}: {e}")\n\n    def add_to_path(self, new_path):\n        """\n        Adds a directory to both system and user PATH environment variables.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isdir(new_path):\n            self.logger.warning(f"Path does not exist, skipping PATH update: {new_path}")\n            return\n\n        # Update system PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                paths = path_value.split(";")\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to system PATH")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to update system PATH. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies.
