Running test as current user only.")\n            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)\n\n        results = []\n        all_success = True\n\n        for user in self.test_users:\n            username = user.get("username")\n            password = user.get("password")\n            if not username or not password:\n                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")\n                results.append({\n                    "username": username,\n                    "success": False,\n                    "error": "Missing credentials"\n                })\n                all_success = False\n                continue\n\n            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")\n            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)\n            results.append(result)\n            if not result["success"]:\n                all_success = False\n\n        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}\n\n    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as the current user.\n        """\n        username = getpass.getuser()\n        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")\n        try:\n            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args\n            proc = subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                check=False\n            )\n            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")\n            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")\n            success = proc.returncode == 0\n            if success:\n                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n            else:\n                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n            return {\n                "success": success,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": output,\n                    "error": error,\n                    "returncode": proc.returncode\n                }]\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")\n            return {\n                "success": False,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": "",\n                    "error": str(e),\n                    "returncode": -1\n                }]\n            }\n\n    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as a different user using \'runas\' and a temporary batch file.\n        Returns a dict with the result.\n        """\n        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")\n            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")\n            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")\n\n            exe_cmd = f\'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"\'\n            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(f\'@echo off\\n{exe_cmd}\\n\')\n\n            # Use \'runas\' to execute the batch file as the target user\n            # Note: Windows \'runas\' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.\n            # To automate, we use \'psexec\' if available, else prompt for password.\n            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")\n            if psexec_path:\n                run_cmd = [\n                    psexec_path,\n                    "-accepteula",\n                    "-u", username,\n                    "-p", password,\n                    "-d",  # Don\'t wait for process to finish (we\'ll poll for output)\n                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    # Wait for output files to be created\n                    waited = 0\n                    while not os.path.exists(output_file) and waited < self.test_timeout:\n                        import time\n                        time.sleep(1)\n                        waited += 1\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }\n            else:\n                # Fallback: runas (interactive, cannot automate password)\n                self.logger.warning("PsExec not found. \'runas\' will prompt for password interactively.")\n                run_cmd = [\n                    "runas",\n                    f"/user:{username}",\n                    f\'cmd.exe /c "{batch_file}"\'\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Set custom environment variables\n        for var, value in self.env_vars.items():\n            self.set_env_var_system(var, value)\n            self.set_env_var_user(var, value)\n\n        # Add Python and dependencies to PATH\n        if self.update_path:\n            self.add_to_path(self.python_install_path)\n            for dep_path in self.dependency_paths:\n                self.add_to_path(dep_path)\n\n        self.logger.info("Environment variable configuration completed.")\n\n    def set_env_var_system(self, var, value):\n        """\n        Sets a system-wide environment variable using Windows registry.\n        """\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE,\n                                r"SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Session Manager\\Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                winreg.SetValueEx(key, var, 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, value)\n            self.logger.info(f"Set system environment variable: {var}={value}")\n        except PermissionError:\n            self.logger.error(f"Permission denied: Unable to set system environment variable {var}. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. Running test as current user only.")\n            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)\n\n        results = []\n        all_success = True\n\n        for user in self.test_users:\n            username = user.get("username")\n            password = user.get("password")\n            if not username or not password:\n                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")\n                results.append({\n                    "username": username,\n                    "success": False,\n                    "error": "Missing credentials"\n                })\n                all_success = False\n                continue\n\n            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")\n            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)\n            results.append(result)\n            if not result["success"]:\n                all_success = False\n\n        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}\n\n    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as the current user.\n        """\n        username = getpass.getuser()\n        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")\n        try:\n            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args\n            proc = subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                check=False\n            )\n            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")\n            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")\n            success = proc.returncode == 0\n            if success:\n                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n            else:\n                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n            return {\n                "success": success,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": output,\n                    "error": error,\n                    "returncode": proc.returncode\n                }]\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")\n            return {\n                "success": False,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": "",\n                    "error": str(e),\n                    "returncode": -1\n                }]\n            }\n\n    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as a different user using \'runas\' and a temporary batch file.\n        Returns a dict with the result.\n        """\n        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")\n            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")\n            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")\n\n            exe_cmd = f\'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"\'\n            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(f\'@echo off\\n{exe_cmd}\\n\')\n\n            # Use \'runas\' to execute the batch file as the target user\n            # Note: Windows \'runas\' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.\n            # To automate, we use \'psexec\' if available, else prompt for password.\n            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")\n            if psexec_path:\n                run_cmd = [\n                    psexec_path,\n                    "-accepteula",\n                    "-u", username,\n                    "-p", password,\n                    "-d",  # Don\'t wait for process to finish (we\'ll poll for output)\n                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    # Wait for output files to be created\n                    waited = 0\n                    while not os.path.exists(output_file) and waited < self.test_timeout:\n                        import time\n                        time.sleep(1)\n                        waited += 1\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }\n            else:\n                # Fallback: runas (interactive, cannot automate password)\n                self.logger.warning("PsExec not found. \'runas\' will prompt for password interactively.")\n                run_cmd = [\n                    "runas",\n                    f"/user:{username}",\n                    f\'cmd.exe /c "{batch_file}"\'\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The tester/tester.py module has been successfully implemented to automate testing of executables across multiple user accounts. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_windows_deployment_tool/utils/logger.py has been successfully created, with content:
import logging
import os
import sys
from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler

def get_logger(log_file="deployment.log", log_level="INFO"):
    """
    Configures and returns a logger with both file and console handlers.
    Supports log levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL.
    Log messages are formatted for troubleshooting.
    """
    logger = logging.getLogger("PythonWindowsDeploymentTool")
    if getattr(logger, "_is_configured", False):
        return logger  # Prevent duplicate handlers

    # Set log level
    level = getattr(logging, log_level.upper(), logging.INFO)
    logger.setLevel(level)

    # Log format
    formatter = logging.Formatter(
        fmt="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
    )

    # Console handler
    console_handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    console_handler.setLevel(level)
    console_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    logger.addHandler(console_handler)

    # File handler (rotating)
    log_dir = os.path.dirname(log_file)
    if log_dir and not os.path.exists(log_dir):
        os.makedirs(log_dir, exist_ok=True)
    file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(log_file, maxBytes=5 * 1024 * 1024, backupCount=3, encoding="utf-8")
    file_handler.setLevel(level)
    file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    logger.addHandler(file_handler)

    logger._is_configured = True
    return logger

Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. logger\n        self.last_exe_path = None\n\n    def compile(self, script_path, packager="pyinstaller"):\n        """\n        Compiles the given Python script into a standalone .exe file using the specified packager.\n        Returns the path to the generated executable, or None on failure.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isfile(script_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Script not found: {script_path}")\n            return None\n\n        output_dir = self.config.get("build_output_dir", "dist")\n        if not os.path.isdir(output_dir):\n            os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)\n\n        if packager.lower() == "pyinstaller":\n            exe_path = self._compile_with_pyinstaller(script_path, output_dir)\n        elif packager.lower() == "cx_freeze":\n            exe_path = self._compile_with_cx_freeze(script_path, output_dir)\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unknown packager: {packager}")\n            return None\n\n        if exe_path and os.path.isfile(exe_path):\n            self.last_exe_path = exe_path\n            self.logger.info(f"Executable created: {exe_path}")\n            return exe_path\n        else:\n            self.logger.error("Failed to create executable.")\n            return None\n\n    def get_last_exe_path(self):\n        """\n        Returns the path to the last successfully built executable.\n        """\n        return self.last_exe_path\n\n    def _compile_with_pyinstaller(self, script_path, output_dir):\n        """\n        Uses PyInstaller to compile the script.\n        """\n        self.logger.info("Compiling with PyInstaller...")\n        exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)\n        cmd = [\n            sys.executable, "-m", "PyInstaller",\n            "--onefile",\n            "--distpath", output_dir,\n            "--name", exe_name,\n            script_path\n        ]\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)\n        build_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "build_cxfreeze")\n        if not os.path.isdir(build_dir):\n            os.makedirs(build_dir, This module should provide methods to run the .exe under different user contexts and collect/report results. I will now create and write tester/tester.py according to the system design.\n\n```json\n[\n    {\n        "command_name": "Engineer2.write_new_code",\n        "args": {\n            "path": "python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py",\n            "file_description": "Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts. Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport tempfile\nimport getpass\nimport shutil\n\nclass Tester:\n    """\n    Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts.\n    Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, config, logger):\n        self.config = config\n        self.logger = logger\n        self.test_users = config.get("test_users", [])  # List of dicts: [{"username": "...", "password": "..."}]\n        self.test_args = config.get("test_args", [])    # Optional arguments to pass to the executable\n        self.test_timeout = config.get("test_timeout", 30)  # Timeout in seconds for each test run\n\n    def test_executable_multiuser(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Tests the given executable across multiple user accounts.\n        Returns a dict with overall success and per-user results.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isfile(exe_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Executable not found: {exe_path}")\n            return {"success": False, "results": []}\n\n        if not self.test_users:\n            self.logger.warning("No test users specified in configuration. Running test as current user only.")\n            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)\n\n        results = []\n        all_success = True\n\n        for user in self.test_users:\n            username = user.get("username")\n            password = user.get("password")\n            if not username or not password:\n                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")\n                results.append({\n                    "username": username,\n                    "success": False,\n                    "error": "Missing credentials"\n                })\n                all_success = False\n                continue\n\n            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")\n            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)\n            results.append(result)\n            if not result["success"]:\n                all_success = False\n\n        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}\n\n    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as the current user.\n        """\n        username = getpass.getuser()\n        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")\n        try:\n            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args\n            proc = subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                check=False\n            )\n            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")\n            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")\n            success = proc.returncode == 0\n            if success:\n                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n            else:\n                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n            return {\n                "success": success,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": output,\n                    "error": error,\n                    "returncode": proc.returncode\n                }]\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")\n            return {\n                "success": False,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": "",\n                    "error": str(e),\n                    "returncode": -1\n                }]\n            }\n\n    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as a different user using \'runas\' and a temporary batch file.\n        Returns a dict with the result.\n        """\n        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")\n            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")\n            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")\n\n            exe_cmd = f\'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"\'\n            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(f\'@echo off\\n{exe_cmd}\\n\')\n\n            # Use \'runas\' to execute the batch file as the target user\n            # Note: Windows \'runas\' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.\n            # To automate, we use \'psexec\' if available, else prompt for password.\n            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")\n            if psexec_path:\n                run_cmd = [\n                    psexec_path,\n                    "-accepteula",\n                    "-u", username,\n                    "-p", password,\n                    "-d",  # Don\'t wait for process to finish (we\'ll poll for output)\n                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    # Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. logger\n        self.last_exe_path = None\n\n    def compile(self, script_path, packager="pyinstaller"):\n        """\n        Compiles the given Python script into a standalone .exe file using the specified packager.\n        Returns the path to the generated executable, or None on failure.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isfile(script_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Script not found: {script_path}")\n            return None\n\n        output_dir = self.config.get("build_output_dir", "dist")\n        if not os.path.isdir(output_dir):\n            os.makedirs(output_dir,    exe_path = self._compile_with_cx_freeze(script_path, output_dir)\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unknown packager: {packager}")\n            return None\n\n        if exe_path and os.path.isfile(exe_path):\n            self.last_exe_path = exe_path\n            self.logger.info(f"Executable created: {exe_path}")\n            return exe_path\n        else:\n            self.logger.error("Failed to create executable.")\n            return None\n\n    def get_last_exe_path(self):\n        """\n        Returns the path to the last successfully built executable.\n        """\n        return self.last_exe_path\n\n    def _compile_with_pyinstaller(self, script_path, output_dir):\n        """\n        Uses PyInstaller to compile the script.\n        """\n        "--distpath", output_dir,\n            "--name", exe_name,\n            script_path\n        ]\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE,     return exe_path\n            else:\n                # Try to find the exe in the output directory\n                for f in os.listdir(output_dir):\n                    if exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)\n        build_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "build_cxfreeze")\n        if not os.path.isdir(build_dir):\n            os.makedirs(build_dir, I will now create and write tester/tester.py according to the system design.\n\n```json\n[\n    {\n        "command_name": "Engineer2.write_new_code",\n        "args": {\n            "path": "python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py",\n            "file_description": "Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts. Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport tempfile\nimport getpass\nimport shutil\n\nclass Tester:\n    """\n    Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts.\n    Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, config, logger):\n        self.config = config\n        self.logger = logger\n        self.test_users = config.get("test_users", [])  # List of dicts: [{"username": "...", "password": "..."}]\n        self.test_args = config.get("test_args", [])    # Optional arguments to pass to the executable\n        self.test_timeout = config.get("test_timeout", 30)  # Timeout in seconds for each test run\n\n    def test_executable_multiuser(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Tests the given executable across multiple user accounts.\n        Returns a dict with overall success and per-user results.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isfile(exe_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Executable not found: {exe_path}")\n            return {"success": False, "results": []}\n\n        if not self.test_users:\n            self.logger.warning("No test users specified in configuration. Running test as current user only.")\n            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)\n\n        results = []\n        all_success = True\n\n        for user in self.test_users:\n            username = user.get("username")\n            password = user.get("password")\n            if not username or not password:\n                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")\n                results.append({\n                    "username": username,\n                    "success": False,\n                    "error": "Missing credentials"\n                })\n                all_success = False\n                continue\n\n            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")\n            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)\n            results.append(result)\n            if not result["success"]:\n                all_success = False\n\n        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}\n\n    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as the current user.\n        """\n        username = getpass.getuser()\n        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")\n        try:\n            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args\n            proc = subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                check=False\n            )\n            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")\n            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")\n            success = proc.returncode == 0\n            if success:\n                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n            else:\n                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n            return {\n                "success": success,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": output,\n                    "error": error,\n                    "returncode": proc.returncode\n                }]\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")\n            return {\n                "success": False,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": "",\n                    "error": str(e),\n                    "returncode": -1\n                }]\n            }\n\n    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as a different user using \'runas\' and a temporary batch file.\n        Returns a dict with the result.\n        """\n        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")\n            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")\n            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")\n\n            exe_cmd = f\'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"\'\n            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(f\'@echo off\\n{exe_cmd}\\n\')\n\n            # Use \'runas\' to execute the batch file as the target user\n            # Note: Windows \'runas\' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.\n            # To automate, we use \'psexec\' if available, else prompt for password.\n            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")\n            if psexec_path:\n                run_cmd = [\n                    psexec_path,\n                    "-accepteula",\n                    "-u", username,\n                    "-p", password,\n                    "-d",  # Don\'t wait for process to finish (we\'ll poll for output)\n                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    # Wait for output files to be created\n                    waited = 0\n                    while not os.path.exists(output_file) and waited < self.test_timeout:\n                        import time\n                        time.sleep(1)\n                        waited += 1\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }\n            else:\n                # Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. self.logger.error("Failed to create executable.")\n            return None\n\n    def get_last_exe_path(self):\n        """\n        Returns the path to the last successfully built executable.\n        """\n        return self.last_exe_path\n\n    def _compile_with_pyinstaller(self, script_path, output_dir):\n        """\n        Uses PyInstaller to compile the script.\n        """\n exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)\n        build_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "build_cxfreeze")\n        if not os.path.isdir(build_dir):\n            os.makedirs(build_dir, I will now create and write tester/tester.py according to the system design.\n\n```json\n[\n    {\n        "command_name": "Engineer2.write_new_code",\n        "args": {\n            "path": "python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py",\n            "file_description": "Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts. Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/python_windows_deployment_tool/tester/tester.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport tempfile\nimport getpass\nimport shutil\n\nclass Tester:\n    """\n    Automates testing of generated executables across multiple user accounts.\n    Provides methods to run the .exe under different user contexts, collect output, and report results.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, config, logger):\n        self.config = config\n        self.logger = logger\n        self.test_users = config.get("test_users", [])  # List of dicts: [{"username": "...", "password": "..."}]\n        self.test_args = config.get("test_args", [])    # Optional arguments to pass to the executable\n        self.test_timeout = config.get("test_timeout", 30)  # Timeout in seconds for each test run\n\n    def test_executable_multiuser(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Tests the given executable across multiple user accounts.\n        Returns a dict with overall success and per-user results.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isfile(exe_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Executable not found: {exe_path}")\n            return {"success": False, "results": []}\n\n        if not self.test_users:\n            self.logger.warning("No test users specified in configuration. Running test as current user only.")\n            return self._test_as_current_user(exe_path)\n\n        results = []\n        all_success = True\n\n        for user in self.test_users:\n            username = user.get("username")\n            password = user.get("password")\n            if not username or not password:\n                self.logger.error(f"Missing username or password for test user: {user}")\n                results.append({\n                    "username": username,\n                    "success": False,\n                    "error": "Missing credentials"\n                })\n                all_success = False\n                continue\n\n            self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as user: {username}")\n            result = self._run_as_user(exe_path, username, password)\n            results.append(result)\n            if not result["success"]:\n                all_success = False\n\n        return {"success": all_success, "results": results}\n\n    def _test_as_current_user(self, exe_path):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as the current user.\n        """\n        username = getpass.getuser()\n        self.logger.info(f"Testing executable as current user: {username}")\n        try:\n            cmd = [exe_path] + self.test_args\n            proc = subprocess.run(\n                cmd,\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                check=False\n            )\n            output = proc.stdout.decode(errors="ignore")\n            error = proc.stderr.decode(errors="ignore")\n            success = proc.returncode == 0\n            if success:\n                self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n            else:\n                self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n            return {\n                "success": success,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": output,\n                    "error": error,\n                    "returncode": proc.returncode\n                }]\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as current user: {e}")\n            return {\n                "success": False,\n                "results": [{\n                    "username": username,\n                    "output": "",\n                    "error": str(e),\n                    "returncode": -1\n                }]\n            }\n\n    def _run_as_user(self, exe_path, username, password):\n        """\n        Runs the executable as a different user using \'runas\' and a temporary batch file.\n        Returns a dict with the result.\n        """\n        # Create a temporary batch file to run the executable and redirect output\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n            output_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "output.txt")\n            error_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "error.txt")\n            batch_file = os.path.join(tmpdir, "run_test.bat")\n\n            exe_cmd = f\'"{exe_path}" {" ".join(self.test_args)} > "{output_file}" 2> "{error_file}"\'\n            with open(batch_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:\n                f.write(f\'@echo off\\n{exe_cmd}\\n\')\n\n            # Use \'runas\' to execute the batch file as the target user\n            # Note: Windows \'runas\' does not accept password via command line for security reasons.\n            # To automate, we use \'psexec\' if available, else prompt for password.\n            psexec_path = shutil.which("psexec")\n            if psexec_path:\n                run_cmd = [\n                    psexec_path,\n                    "-accepteula",\n                    "-u", username,\n                    "-p", password,\n                    "-d",  # Don\'t wait for process to finish (we\'ll poll for output)\n                    "cmd.exe", "/c", batch_file\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    # Wait for output files to be created\n                    waited = 0\n                    while not os.path.exists(output_file) and waited < self.test_timeout:\n                        import time\n                        time.sleep(1)\n                        waited += 1\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Error:\\n{error}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": success,\n                        "output": output,\n                        "error": error,\n                        "returncode": proc.returncode\n                    }\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.logger.error(f"Exception during test as user {username}: {e}")\n                    return {\n                        "username": username,\n                        "success": False,\n                        "output": "",\n                        "error": str(e),\n                        "returncode": -1\n                    }\n            else:\n                # Fallback: runas (interactive, cannot automate password)\n                self.logger.warning("PsExec not found. \'runas\' will prompt for password interactively.")\n                run_cmd = [\n                    "runas",\n                    f"/user:{username}",\n                    f\'cmd.exe /c "{batch_file}"\'\n                ]\n                try:\n                    proc = subprocess.run(\n                        run_cmd,\n                        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n                        timeout=self.test_timeout,\n                        check=False\n                    )\n                    output = ""\n                    error = ""\n                    if os.path.exists(output_file):\n                        with open(output_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            output = f.read()\n                    if os.path.exists(error_file):\n                        with open(error_file, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:\n                            error = f.read()\n                    success = proc.returncode == 0 and not error\n                    if success:\n                        self.logger.info(f"Test succeeded for user {username}. Output:\\n{output}")\n                    else:\n                        self.logger.error(f"Test failed for user {username}. Official Python download URLs\n        base_url = f"https://www.python.org/ftp/python/{self.python_version}/"\n        if self.architecture == "amd64":\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}-amd64.exe"\n        else:\n            filename = f"python-{self.python_version}.exe"\n        return base_url + filename\n\n    def is_python_installed(self):\n        """\n        Checks if the required Python version is installed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Try to find python executable in install_path\n            python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n            if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n                output = subprocess.check_output([python_exe, "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n                version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n                if version_str == self.python_version:\n                    self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found at {python_exe}")\n                    return True\n            # Try system python\n            output = subprocess.check_output(["python", "--version"], stderr=subprocess.STDOUT)\n            version_str = output.decode().strip().split()[-1]\n            if version_str == self.python_version:\n                self.logger.info(f"Python {self.python_version} found in system PATH")\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Python version check failed: {e}")\n        return False\n\n    def install_python(self):\n        """\n        Downloads and installs Python if not already installed.\n        """\n        self.logger.info(f"Preparing to install Python {self.python_version} ({self.architecture})")\n        # Download installer\n        if not os.path.isfile(self.installer_filename):\n            self.logger.info(f"Downloading Python installer from {self.installer_url}")\n            try:\n                with requests.get(self.installer_url, stream=True, timeout=60) as r:\n                    r.raise_for_status()\n                    with open(self.installer_filename, "wb") as f:\n                        for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                            f.write(chunk)\n                self.logger.info(f"Downloaded installer to {self.installer_filename}")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.error(f"Failed to download Python installer: {e}")\n                return False\n        else:\n            self.logger.info(f"Installer already downloaded: {self.installer_filename}")\n\n        # Run installer silently\n        install_args = [\n            self.installer_filename,\n            "/quiet",\n            "InstallAllUsers=1",\n            f"TargetDir={self.install_path}",\n            "PrependPath=1",\n            "Include_pip=1"\n        ]\n        self.logger.info(f"Running installer: {\' \'.join(install_args)}")\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(install_args, check=True)\n            self.logger.info("Python installation completed.")\n        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python installer failed: {e}")\n            return False\n\n        # Verify installation\n        python_exe = os.path.join(self.install_path, "python.exe")\n        if os.path.isfile(python_exe):\n            self.logger.info(f"Python installed at {python_exe}")\n            return True\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Python executable not found after installation: {python_exe}")\n            return False'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The installer/installer.py module has been successfully implemented, handling Python installation on Windows with architecture and version detection. Run as administrator.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update system PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update user PATH\n        try:\n            with winreg.OpenKey(winreg.HKEY_CURRENT_USER,\n                                r"Environment",\n                                0, winreg.KEY_READ | winreg.KEY_SET_VALUE) as key:\n                try:\n                    path_value, _ = winreg.QueryValueEx(key, "Path")\n                except FileNotFoundError:\n                    path_value = ""\n                paths = path_value.split(";") if path_value else []\n                if new_path not in paths:\n                    paths.append(new_path)\n                    new_path_value = ";".join(paths)\n                    winreg.SetValueEx(key, "Path", 0, winreg.REG_EXPAND_SZ, new_path_value)\n                    self.logger.info(f"Added {new_path} to user PATH")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update user PATH: {e}")\n\n        # Update current process PATH for immediate effect\n        os.environ["PATH"] = os.environ.get("PATH", "") + ";" + new_path\n\n    def refresh_environment(self):\n        """\n        Notifies the system that environment variables have changed.\n        """\n        try:\n            # Broadcast WM_SETTINGCHANGE to all windows\n            import ctypes\n            HWND_BROADCAST = 0xFFFF\n            WM_SETTINGCHANGE = 0x001A\n            result = ctypes.windll.user32.SendMessageTimeoutW(\n                HWND_BROADCAST, WM_SETTINGCHANGE, 0, "Environment", 0x0002, 5000, None\n            )\n            self.logger.info("Broadcasted environment change to system.")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.warning(f"Failed to broadcast environment change: {e}")'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The env/env_manager.py module has been successfully implemented to configure system and user environment variables for Python and dependencies. logger\n        self.last_exe_path = None\n\n    def compile(self, script_path, packager="pyinstaller"):\n        """\n        Compiles the given Python script into a standalone .exe file using the specified packager.\n        Returns the path to the generated executable, or None on failure.\n        """\n        if not os.path.isfile(script_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Script not found: {script_path}")\n            return None\n\n        output_dir = self.config.get("build_output_dir", "dist")\n        if not os.path.isdir(output_dir):\n            os.makedirs(output_dir,    exe_path = self._compile_with_cx_freeze(script_path, output_dir)\n        else:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unknown packager: {packager}")\n            return None\n\n        if exe_path and os.path.isfile(exe_path):\n            self.last_exe_path = exe_path\n            self.logger.info(f"Executable created: {exe_path}")\n            return exe_path\n        else:\n            self.logger.error("Failed to create executable.")\n            return None\n\n    def get_last_exe_path(self):\n        """\n        Returns the path to the last successfully built executable.\n        """\n        return self.last_exe_path\n\n    def _compile_with_pyinstaller(self, script_path, output_dir):\n        """\n        Uses PyInstaller to compile the script.\n        """\n        "--distpath", output_dir,\n            "--name", exe_name,\n            script_path\n        ]\n        try:\n            result = subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE,     return exe_path\n            else:\n                # Try to find the exe in the output directory\n                for f in os.listdir(output_dir):\n                    if exe_name = self._get_exe_name(script_path)\n        build_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "build_cxfreeze")\n        if not os.path.isdir(build_dir):\n            os.makedirs(build_dir,
